title: 3D Printing
heading: 3D Printing
text: 3D printing turns digital models into physical parts by laying down thin layers of material (most DSPACE quests use FDM printers with PLA).

title: 3D Printing
heading: What you learn
text: - How to set up and calibrate an entry-level FDM printer - How to handle first-layer adhesion, retraction, and temperature tuning - How to keep prints consistent with maintenance tasks like belt tensioning and nozzle cleaning

title: 3D Printing
heading: Quest trailheads
text: - Set up your first 3D printer - Level the Print Bed - Print a Calibration Cube - Print a Temperature Tower - Tune Retraction Settings - Print a Phone Stand

title: 3D Printing
heading: Key gear and materials
text: - Entry-level FDM printer, spare nozzles, and a print removal scraper - PLA filament (green and white spools are stocked in inventory) - Digital calipers for checking dimensions and squareness

title: 3D Printing
heading: Good practices
text: - Run calibration prints (Benchy, temperature tower) before long jobs. - Keep your nozzle clean and watch for signs of a clog or "blob of death" before it ruins a print. - Record slicer settings in your quest notes so you can repeat successful builds.

title: About
heading: About DSPACE
text:
DSPACE is an incremental simulation game about resource management and the practical skills behind space exploration. The current gameplay is intentionally grounded: you learn real-world systems on Earth first, then build toward the space era as new milestones ship. In the game, you will learn skills, work on quests, and grow a resilient technical foundation.
 The game is still under active development. If you would like to get involved in the creation of the game, please join the Discord and say hello!

title: About
heading: Start with the basics
text: Learn skills related to resource management, energy, fabrication, and life support. Current quests center on grounded systems like electricity, solar, aquaria, hydroponics, chemistry, electronics, and operations, so you build a working toolkit before heading off-world.

title: About
heading: Gain momentum
text: Build confidence by assembling and validating real infrastructure: power systems, tools, monitoring, and small-scale manufacturing. The game is expanding bottom-up, so space-based orbital and suborbital launches are not playable yet; today’s rocketry content is educational, model-rocket scale. When we introduce launch systems, you will already have the energy, materials, and operational habits needed to support them.

title: About
heading: Research
text: In-situ resource utilization (ISRU), lunar mining, and deep-space fuel production are long-term goals. We are laying the groundwork first with terrestrial resource loops, storage, and process chains so that future off-world systems feel earned rather than assumed.

title: About
heading: Create your own content
text:
DSPACE is designed as an extensible platform where you can create and contribute:
 - Custom Quests - Create and submit missions with the in-game editor - Custom Items - Create virtual resources, tools, and components - Custom Processes - Build activities that transform or utilize items
 Our Content Development Guide provides a comprehensive overview of all content creation options, workflows, and best practices.

title: About
heading: Educational Foundation
text:
All content in DSPACE is designed with education in mind. Through gameplay, you'll learn about:
 - Sustainable resource management - Closed-loop life support systems - Energy generation and storage - Manufacturing techniques relevant to space - Biology and agriculture in controlled environments - Physics principles that govern space exploration

title: About
heading: Where to go next
text: Check out the mission statement and take a look at the roadmap. If you'd like to contribute, visit our contribution guide.

title: Achievements
heading: Achievements
text: Achievements celebrate major milestones in your save file. They unlock the first time you meet the listed requirements and update automatically whenever your quest log or inventory changes. Each entry in the Achievements menu tracks progress toward its goal so you can see how close you are to the next badge.

title: Achievements
heading: Current achievements
text:
- First Steps – Complete your first quest. - Trailblazer – Finish five quests to set your course. - Mission Specialist – Finish 10 quests to prove your dedication. - Quest Navigator – Complete 20 quests across the DSPACE atlas. - Quest Vanguard – Finish 30 quests and become a veteran explorer. - Energy Investor – Store a combined total of 500 dWatt in your inventory. - Grid Steward – Build a 2,000 dWatt energy reserve. - Grid Magnate – Reach a 5,000 dWatt stockpile. - Solar Scout – Secure a portable solar panel for field ops. - Solar Architect – Assemble a full 1 kWh solar setup. - Wind Pioneer – Add a 500 W wind turbine to your base. - Maker Online – Bring an entry-level FDM 3D printer into service. - Calibration Captain – Print a Benchy to prove your setup is dialed in. - Launch Ready – Complete a launch-capable model rocket build. - Guidance Officer – Assemble a guided flight stack for precision launches. - Hydroponics Starter – Deploy your first hydroponics kit. - Green Thumb – Harvest a bundle of basil leaves from your grow. - Aquarium Architect – Set up a 150 L aquarium habitat. - Stellar Cartographer – Capture a stacked star trail photo. - Mission Recorder – Open a mission logbook to track your progress.
 Visit /achievements to view your progress. Cards update in real time as you finish quests, generate dWatt, or pick up new equipment. Progress is stored locally alongside your game state, so achievements persist between sessions without sending any data to a server.

title: Amazing
heading: Amazing
text:
Amazing is the in-lore distribution layer that fulfills item buys and sells, connecting buyers and sellers. The entire organization is automated by AI and robots, and it is verifiably carbon negative. Transportation and logistics are handled by a fleet of autonomous vehicles and drones, ensuring efficient and environmentally conscious delivery of goods and services.
 Right now, Amazing is mostly world-building. The game does not yet use it as a mechanic: items bought or sold in /inventory are added or removed immediately without any shipping delay. In the future, we may add a realtime shipping mechanic (see /docs/realtime).
 Amazing is designed to be a hyperstructure. It is a protocol that is unstoppable, free, valuable, expansive, permissionless, positive sum, and credibly neutral.

title: Amazing
heading: How it works
text: .amazing { max-width: 100%; width: auto; height: auto; }

title: Aquaria
heading: Aquaria
text: Aquaria quests focus on building and caring for freshwater tanks, from Walstad setups to steady fish husbandry. The skills emphasize water quality, temperature stability, and gentle maintenance routines.

title: Aquaria
heading: What you learn
text: - How to set up and cycle a tank (filters, heaters, and lighting) - How to test pH, ammonia, nitrite, and nitrate levels - How to plan regular water changes and keep logs

title: Aquaria
heading: Quest trailheads
text: - Set up a Walstad tank - Install an aquarium heater - Install an aquarium light - Test water parameters - Perform a partial water change - Add guppies

title: Aquaria
heading: Key gear
text: - Aquarium (150 L) with a stable stand and aquarium gravel (1 kg) for substrate - Aquarium heater (150 W), aquarium LED light (20 W), and aquarium thermometer (0–50°C) - Aquarium liquid test kit, water conditioner, and a water test logbook - Gravel vacuum and aquarium net for maintenance days

title: Aquaria
heading: Common pitfalls
text: - Adding fish before the tank has cycled or before water parameters are stable - Skipping dechlorination or mismatching water temperatures during changes - Replacing filter media all at once instead of rinsing and reusing it

title: Aquaria
heading: Next steps
text: - Keep weekly logs so you can compare readings after each quest cycle. - Pair tank monitoring with data tracking practices from Programming.

title: Astronomy & Observation
heading: Astronomy & Observation
text: Astronomy quests teach you to observe the sky, build simple optics, and document what you see. Expect a mix of visual observation, photography, and light-pollution tracking.

title: Astronomy & Observation
heading: What you learn
text: - How to build a simple telescope from lenses and a tube - How to locate bright targets like the Moon, ISS, and planets - How to capture photos and star trails with stable mounts

title: Astronomy & Observation
heading: Quest trailheads
text: - Assemble a Simple Telescope - Locate the North Star - Map the Constellations - Track Jupiter's Moons - Photograph the ISS - Capture Star Trails

title: Astronomy & Observation
heading: Key gear
text: - Basic telescope or DIY optics with a 50 mm magnifying lens and 20 mm magnifying lens - Planisphere star chart and red flashlight to preserve night vision - Camera tripod for long exposures and stable sketches

title: Astronomy & Observation
heading: Common pitfalls
text: - Observing before your eyes adapt to the dark or without a red light - Forgetting to stabilize the tripod, leading to blurry star trails - Not noting timestamps or sky conditions, which makes comparisons harder later

title: Astronomy & Observation
heading: Next steps
text: - Build a repeatable observation log and archive photos from each session. - Use Programming to chart your observation cadence and results.

title: Authentication Flow
heading: Authentication Flow
text: DSPACE uses GitHub authentication only for features that talk directly to GitHub. Most of the game works offline. Authentication is required when you submit content to the official repository, sync your save data with Cloud Sync, or choose quest options that are gated by GitHub access.

title: Authentication Flow
heading: Where you'll see authentication prompts
text:
Authentication appears in the following places today:
 - Quest submissions at (creates a pull request in the repo). - Custom content bundle submissions at (creates a pull request with bundle JSON). - Cloud Sync at (reads/writes private GitHub gists for backups). - Quest options that require GitHub (options flagged with stay disabled until a valid token is saved). - Settings → Log out (clears saved GitHub credentials, Cloud Sync gist ID, and avatar URL from this device).

title: Authentication Flow
heading: Personal Access Tokens
text:
To authenticate, generate a GitHub personal access token. The token is used client-side only and never sent anywhere except directly to GitHub's API.
 1. Visit GitHub Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens. 2. Create a token with the scopes you need (see below). 3. Paste the token into the relevant form in DSPACE and click Save.

title: Authentication Flow
heading: Token scopes by feature
text:
- Quest submissions ( ): needs repository access for the DSPACE repo. Classic PATs should include . Fine-grained tokens should grant read/write access to the DSPACE repository (contents + pull requests). - Bundle submissions ( ): same requirements as quest submissions. - Cloud Sync ( ): needs access (read/write) to create and list private gists. You can use a separate gist-only token if you prefer.
 If you want a single token for all surfaces, a classic PAT with + is sufficient.

title: Authentication Flow
heading: Token Storage
text: Your token is stored in IndexedDB under so you don't need to re-enter it each time you open the game. Cloud Sync also stores the selected gist ID at . You can clear the saved token using the Clear button next to the input field.

title: Authentication Flow
heading: Security Considerations
text:
- The token never leaves your browser except when making authenticated requests to GitHub. - You can revoke the token at any time from your GitHub settings. - If you share your device, remember to clear the token to prevent unauthorized submissions. - User-generated markdown is sanitized with DOMPurify before rendering to prevent XSS.
 With a valid token saved, features like quest submission, bundle submission, gated quest options, and Cloud Sync will work seamlessly.

title: Authentication Flow
heading: Logging out
text: If you're using a shared machine, open the Settings page and click Log out to clear the saved GitHub token, Cloud Sync gist ID, and stored from the device. This removes access to your synced data until you sign in again.

title: Backups
heading: Backups
text: DSPACE offers backup options that protect both your overall game state and any custom content you create. This guide explains what gets saved, where the data lives, and how to restore it on another device.

title: Backups
heading: What gets backed up
text:
- Game saves – quest progress, inventory, and processes. - Custom content – player-created items, quests, and processes. - Cloud Sync (optional) – automatic encrypted cloud storage for the above data. Cloud Sync is covered in more detail at Cloud Sync.
 Backups use a stable JSON schema so exports from older releases remain compatible with newer clients.

title: Backups
heading: Exporting and importing game saves
text:
1. Open Import/export gamesaves. 2. Click Copy to place a Base64-encoded JSON snapshot on your clipboard (works even if the Clipboard API is unavailable) or download the text manually. 3. To restore, paste a previously saved string and select Import. Your quests, inventory, and processes will be replaced with the imported data.
 The encoded JSON stores , , and keys. Keep the output somewhere you control (password manager, notes app, or version-controlled gist).

title: Backups
heading: Exporting and importing custom content
text:
1. Open Custom Content Backup. 2. Click Copy to export only user-created items, quests, and processes as a Base64-encoded JSON string. 3. To restore, paste the saved string and choose Import.
 The encoded JSON contains , , and keys. Use this path if you want to move custom creations between profiles without touching your main save.

title: Backups
heading: Automated backups with Cloud Sync
text: For hands-off backups, enable Cloud Sync from Settings (see the dedicated Cloud Sync doc). Once configured, the client periodically uploads encrypted snapshots so you do not need to manage strings manually. Local exports remain available as a manual failsafe.

title: Backups
heading: Best practices
text: - Version regularly: Export after finishing a quest chain or before testing mods. - Store safely: Treat exports like save files—keep them in a private repo or password manager. - Validate imports: After restoring on a new device, open and to confirm expected progress and items. - Keep both modes: Even with Cloud Sync enabled, keep occasional manual exports so you have an offline backup.

title: Backups
heading: Troubleshooting
text: - Import fails: Ensure the string is complete (no trimmed whitespace) and comes from the matching export path (gamesaves vs. custom content). - Old backup, new client: The schema is backward compatible. If a restore still fails, try a newer backup or contact support via the in-app Help → Contact support option. - Clipboard blocked: Manually select and copy the text area contents; the export remains valid.

title: Chemistry Lab Basics
heading: Chemistry Lab Basics
text: A compact home lab lets you extract useful compounds, tune nutrient solutions, and practice safe chemistry skills.

title: Chemistry Lab Basics
heading: Core tools and supplies
text: - Beakers, graduated cylinders, funnels, and glass stir rods - Safety goggles, lab coat, and spill tray for containment - pH test supplies, buffer ingredients, and dilution containers

title: Chemistry Lab Basics
heading: Quest trailheads
text: - Measure Solution pH - Adjust Solution pH - Dilute Hydrochloric Acid Safely - Neutralize an Acid Spill - Extract Stevia Sweetener - Refine Stevia Crystals

title: Chemistry Lab Basics
heading: Safety tips
text: 1. Wear eye protection and gloves when handling acids or hot liquids. 2. Work in a well-ventilated area and keep an exit path clear. 3. Always add acid to water when diluting, never the reverse.

title: Chemistry Lab Basics
heading: Stevia extraction overview
text: Homegrown stevia leaves contain steviol glycosides. Dry the leaves, steep them in hot water, filter out the solids, and refine the extract into crystals. The Refine Stevia Crystals quest walks through the full process.

title: Cloud Sync
heading: Cloud Sync
text:
DSPACE can back up your progress to a private GitHub gist.
 1. Generate a GitHub personal access token with the scope (see GitHub's token guide). 2. Enter the token in the form and click Save. 3. Click Upload to create/update the gist and store your save data. 4. Copy the resulting Gist ID to use on other devices. 5. On another device, enter the same token and Gist ID and click Download.

title: Cloud Sync
heading: Enable Cloud Sync
text: 1. Create a new secret gist on GitHub. 2. Copy the gist URL or ID. 3. In DSPACE, open the Settings page and provide a GitHub token with gist scope. 4. Enter your gist ID to start syncing.

title: Cloud Sync
heading: Token storage
text: The token and gist ID are stored in IndexedDB and load after the game initialises. Remove them at any time from the Settings page.

title: Cloud Sync
heading: What gets synced?
text:
- Game save data - Custom quests, items, and processes
 Syncs happen in the background whenever changes occur.

title: Cloud Sync
heading: Disable Cloud Sync
text: Use the Log out button on the Settings page to clear the token and gist ID. Local progress remains on your device.

title: Composting
heading: Composting
text: Composting quests teach you to turn organic scraps into stable soil amendments. The skill focuses on the bucket compost method, which is compact and measurable.

title: Composting
heading: What you learn
text: - How to balance greens and browns for a healthy compost mix - How to monitor moisture and temperature in the pile - How to turn and sift finished compost for garden use

title: Composting
heading: Quest trailheads
text: - Start a Compost Bucket - Check Compost Temperature - Turn Your Compost Bucket - Sift Finished Compost

title: Composting
heading: Key gear
text: - 5 gallon bucket with airflow holes for a compact compost vessel - Compost thermometer and compost moisture meter for core readings - Compost temperature log and compost moisture log to track trends - Compost sifter to screen finished material

title: Composting
heading: Common pitfalls
text: - Running too wet or too dry (aim for the "wrung sponge" feel) - Skipping turns, which slows decomposition and creates odors - Using compost before it cools and cures fully

title: Composting
heading: Next steps
text: - Aim for a screened compost blend before top-dressing beds or pots. - Use the same logging habits in Hydroponics and other grow skills.

title: Content Development Guide
heading: DSPACE Content Development Guide
text: DSPACE is designed to be an extensible platform where community members can create and contribute various types of content. This guide serves as the central hub for all content development documentation, providing links to detailed guidelines for each content type.

title: Content Development Guide
heading: Overview
text:
The DSPACE ecosystem supports several types of custom content:
 1. Quests: Interactive missions that guide players through educational activities 2. Items: Virtual resources, tools, and components used throughout the game 3. Processes: Activities that transform, create, or utilize items
 Each content type follows specific guidelines to ensure consistency, educational value, and alignment with DSPACE's mission of democratizing space exploration through practical, hands-on learning.
 Management pages ( , , and ) let you edit or delete custom entries stored locally in IndexedDB.

title: Content Development Guide
heading: In-game custom content editors (current behavior)
text:
Use the in-game editors to create and update custom quests, items, and processes. These editors write to your browser's IndexedDB-backed custom content database (with an in-memory fallback if IndexedDB is unavailable, which means the data will not persist after refresh).
 Create new content
 1. Open the editor: - Quests: - Items: - Processes: 2. Fill out the required fields. Each form validates required fields before saving. 3. Save the entry to store it locally. The editor shows a success message with a link to view the new item, process, or quest.
 Edit existing content
 1. Open the management page: - Quests: - Items: - Processes: 2. Click Edit on the custom entry (built-in content is read-only). 3. The edit routes resolve to , , and . 4. Update the fields and save. The entry is updated in the same IndexedDB store.
 Quest editor specifics
 - Title (min 3 chars) and description (min 10 chars) are required, and titles must be unique across existing quests. - Dialogue nodes need unique IDs, non-empty text, and at least one option. Options can be goto, finish, process, or grantsItems. - Goto options must point to an existing node ID. - Process options require a process ID. - Required or granted item rows need an item ID and a positive count. - The NPC picker uses the built-in catalog; if you load a quest with a custom NPC value, the select includes a “Custom (…)” entry for it. Blank NPC values are defaulted to Mission Control. - Quest images are stored as data URLs and are downsampled to square JPEGs (target 50KB) before saving. If you leave the image blank on a new quest, the editor uses .
 Item editor specifics
 - Items require a name, description, and image; images are stored as compressed JPEG data URLs. - Optional fields include price, unit, type, and dependencies (comma- or newline-separated IDs). - Saving a new item adds it to your local inventory catalog with a custom category label.
 Process editor specifics
 - Processes require a title, duration, and at least one item relationship (requires/consumes/creates). - Durations accept shorthand like , , or and are normalized after saving (for example, ). - Item counts must be positive, and empty rows are ignored on save.
 Storage details
 Custom content persists to the IndexedDB database. The schema includes:
 - Object stores: , , , and (each entity store uses as the key). - stores the key.
 When custom content is saved through the editors:
 - IDs are generated with (or a UUID fallback) when missing. - and are set on new records; is set on edits. - Quests default to , to an empty string, and to if those values are missing. - Items default to when not provided. - Processes default to seconds if missing (the editor provides a normalized duration).
 If IndexedDB is unavailable, custom content falls back to an in-memory store (not localStorage), which is cleared on refresh.
 Export + backup
 Custom quests, items, and processes can be exported via the custom content backup page at , which prepares a JSON bundle for download (used for submissions and backups).

Before creating any content, we recommend:
 1. Play the game: Familiarize yourself with existing content and game mechanics 2. Join the community: Discuss your ideas on Discord 3. Review guidelines: Read the specific guidelines for your content type 4. Start small: Begin with a simple creation before tackling more complex projects

title: Content Development Guide
heading: Quest Development Guidelines
text:
Comprehensive instructions for creating engaging, educational quests that guide players through practical space-related activities. Topics include:
 - Quest philosophy and educational goals - Quest categories and progression paths - Dialogue structure and NPC interactions - Technical requirements and JSON structure - Submission and review process - Example JSON in the Quest Template - Field definitions in the Quest Schema Requirements - Step-by-step Quest Submission Guide - Full workflow in the Quest Contribution Guidelines

title: Content Development Guide
heading: Item Development Guidelines

Detailed guide for creating virtual items that represent resources, tools, and components. Topics include:
 - Item categories and classification - Properties and attributes - Relationship to processes and quests - Best practices for naming and descriptions - Technical implementation details

title: Content Development Guide
heading: Process Development Guidelines

Instructions for creating processes that transform or utilize items. Topics include:
 - Process structure and duration formatting - Item requirements, consumption, and production - Balancing time and rewards - Process states and lifecycle - Creating process chains for progression

title: Content Development Guide
heading: Custom Content Bundles
text: Bundle related quests, items, and processes into a single JSON file to keep submissions in sync.

title: Content Development Guide
heading: AI Assistance for Content Creation
text:
For contributors who want to leverage artificial intelligence in their content creation process, we provide Quest Prompts, Item Prompts and Process Prompts that can be used with modern AI assistants. For automating backlog tasks, see the Codex Implementation Prompt; it walks Codex through selecting an unchecked item from the latest changelog and implementing it from start to finish. For general repository maintenance, the Codex Upgrade Prompt instructs Codex to scan the project for improvements and implement them automatically.
 These guides include:
 - Effective prompt templates for different content types - Best practices for working with AI assistants - Scientific accuracy verification techniques - Dialogue refinement strategies - Full examples of AI-assisted content creation. Our Playwright test walks through creating the quest end to end.
 These prompt engineering templates were initially created to help develop the first batches of quests for DSPACE and can be valuable tools for brainstorming ideas, refining dialogue, and ensuring scientific accuracy in your own content.

title: Content Development Guide
heading: Development Workflow
text:
The general workflow for content development in DSPACE is:
 1. Ideation: Develop your concept based on educational principles 2. Local Development: Create your content following the appropriate guidelines 3. Testing: Verify functionality in your local environment 4. Submission: Submit your content for review via pull request 5. Revision: Address feedback from reviewers 6. Integration: Once approved, your content becomes part of the game

title: Content Development Guide
heading: Testing Your Content
text:
Before submitting:
 - Technical validation: Ensure your content follows all required formats - Educational value: Verify that your content teaches meaningful concepts - Progression fit: Check that your content fits logically in existing progression paths - Consistency: Maintain consistency with existing game content - Balance: Ensure appropriate difficulty and reward balance

 - Follow the Developer Guide - Start with small enhancements before proposing major changes

- Discord Community: Discuss ideas and get feedback - GitHub Repository: View source code and submit changes - Documentation: Browse all game documentation - Contribution Guide: General contribution guidelines
 By following these guidelines, you'll create high-quality content that enhances the DSPACE experience while contributing to our mission of democratizing space exploration through practical, hands-on education. Before submitting, run , , , and to keep the codebase healthy.

title: Custom Quest System
heading: Submitting quests
text:
When your quest is ready to share:
 1. Export your quest JSON from (Prepare backup → Download backup) 2. Paste the quest JSON into (or the bundle JSON into ) 3. Enter a GitHub token to open a pull request
 See the Quest Submission Guide for step-by-step instructions.

title: dCarbon
heading: dCarbon
text:
dCarbon represents the amount of carbon dioxide produced by a player in the game. 1 dCarbon = 1 kg of carbon dioxide. This virtual measurement helps track environmental impact and encourages players to seek cleaner energy solutions.
 This measurement increases when you generate energy from non-renewable sources, unless that energy source explicitly doesn't release carbon dioxide. Players are encouraged to find ways to minimize their dCarbon footprint through sustainable energy solutions.
 Currently, the only way to generate dCarbon is by drawing power from your outlet using your smart plug:

title: dCarbon
heading: Reducing your dCarbon footprint
text:
You can burn down accumulated dCarbon by converting it to dOffset. The conversion process lives in the Processes tab:
 - Run the dCarbon to dOffset process to exchange batches of dCarbon. - Each run consumes dCarbon and a small amount of dUSD to retire the emissions and mint dOffset in return.

title: dOffset
heading: dOffset
text: dOffset represents retired carbon emissions in the game. You earn dOffset by paying down your dCarbon footprint, turning emissions into verified offsets you can track in your inventory.

title: dOffset
heading: How to get dOffset
text:
The primary way to mint dOffset is through the dCarbon to dOffset process:
 - Spend batches of dCarbon along with a small amount of dUSD. - Receive dOffset in return, signaling that the emissions have been retired.
 Reducing dCarbon at the source is still the fastest path. Generating dWatt with renewables like solar or wind keeps your dCarbon low so you need fewer offsets overall.

title: dWatt
heading: dWatt
text:
dWatt represents 1 Watt of power generated in the game. It's a virtual unit used to track your power generation capabilities and achievements. This measurement helps you understand your energy production efficiency and progress in the game.
 In order to perform any process, you must have enough dWatt to power the process. To generate dWatt, see the Electricity page.
 Generating dWatt using an outlet assumes the use of fossil fuels. The in-game smart plug handles this accounting by tracking your draw over time, minting dWatt, and accruing the matching dCarbon. You can later retire those emissions by converting dCarbon into dOffset.
 Renewable sources like solar panels and wind turbines generate dWatt without adding dCarbon, so pairing them with batteries is the cleanest way to keep your power reserves topped up.

title: Docs search
heading: Docs search
text: Use the search box on the page to find documentation by title or by the words inside each page.

title: Docs search
heading: How it works
text: - Title + content search: Typing a word like matches docs whose titles or body text contain that word. - Feature filters: Queries that include must use or to filter by those features. The filter works alongside the text query, and snippets are hidden for these operator searches.

title: Docs search
heading: Snippets
text:
When a match comes from the body text, the docs list shows a short snippet under the link:
 - The snippet shows the first matching word for the query. - It includes up to two words before and two words after the match. - The matched word is bolded.
 The snippet updates as you type and disappears when the search box is cleared.
 Example long URL: https://github.com/democratizedspace/dspace/blob/v3/frontend/src/components/svelte/DocsIndex.svelte

title: dUSD
heading: dUSD
text: dUSD is the in-game equivalent of 1 USD. It cannot be traded for IRL currency, but it can be used to buy things in the game. It is earned by selling items, completing quests, and other tasks.

title: Electricity
heading: Electricity
text: Electricity is the backbone of DSPACE's economy and automation loop. Using it efficiently keeps costs low, while generating it cleanly earns you dWatt without dCarbon penalties.

title: Electricity
heading: Non-renewable energy (dCarbon impact)
text: If you pull power from fossil-fuel sources, DSPACE applies a built-in carbon tax. For every 1,000 dCarbon generated, shop sell prices drop by 1% (up to a 90% reduction). The goal is simple: reward renewable energy and make wasteful usage expensive.

title: Electricity
heading: Renewable energy and storage
text: - Generate dWatt via solar panels and wind turbines, then store it in batteries for later use. - Use charge controllers, inverters, and smart plugs to monitor consumption and protect hardware. - Keep a GFCI tester on hand when working around mains power.

title: Electricity
heading: Quest trailheads
text: - Install a Power Inverter - Maintain Your Battery Pack - Check a battery pack's voltage - Test a GFCI Outlet - Charge a Device Off-Grid

title: Electricity
heading: Safety reminders
text: - Always fuse DC runs close to the battery and keep wire gauges appropriate for the load. - Verify polarity before powering up new circuits.

title: Electronics
heading: Electronics
text: Electronics quests cover sensing, soldering, and measurement skills that power robotics, energy systems, and data logging.

title: Electronics
heading: What you learn
text: - How to build LED circuits and voltage dividers - How to read sensors like thermistors and photoresistors - How to solder, desolder, and validate continuity with a multimeter

title: Electronics
heading: Quest trailheads
text: - Build a basic LED circuit - Check LED polarity with a multimeter - Measure a Resistor - Solder a Wire Connection - Read a Thermistor - Build a Light Sensor

title: Electronics
heading: Key gear
text: - Digital multimeter, soldering iron kit, and solderless breadboard - Lead-free solder spool, solder wick, and desoldering pump for rework - 220 Ohm Resistor, Thermistor (10k NTC), and Photoresistor for sensor builds

title: Electronics
heading: Common pitfalls
text: - Powering circuits before checking polarity or continuity - Creating cold solder joints by moving parts before they cool - Forgetting current limits on LEDs and sensor dividers

title: Electronics
heading: Next steps
text: - Take your sensor circuits into Programming quests for logging. - Combine actuators and sensors in Robotics.

title: Energy Systems
heading: Energy Systems
text: Energy systems quests expand beyond individual solar panels to cover storage, conversion, and off-grid reliability.

title: Energy Systems
heading: What you learn
text: - How to size batteries and inverters for real loads - How to wire charge controllers and protect DC runs - How to blend solar, wind, and manual generation for redundancy

title: Energy Systems
heading: Quest trailheads
text: - Configure a Solar Charge Controller - Install a Power Inverter - Maintain Your Battery Pack - Install a Wind Turbine - Charge a Device Off-Grid - Build a Hand Crank Generator

title: Energy Systems
heading: Key gear
text: - 200 Wh battery pack or 1 kWh battery pack for storage capacity goals - 300 W pure sine inverter and Solar charge controller for conversion and charging - Portable solar panel, 500 W wind turbine, and hand crank generator assembly for sources

title: Energy Systems
heading: Common pitfalls
text: - Mixing battery chemistries or voltages without matching charge profiles - Skipping fuses or undervaluing cable size on DC runs - Tracking generation but not logging load usage over time

title: Energy Systems
heading: Next steps
text: - Deepen production skills with Solar power. - Review safety practices in Electricity.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: What is DSPACE?
text: DSPACE is an incremental simulation game about resource management and space exploration. It focuses on learning by doing through quests, processes, and real-world inspired projects. For a full overview, start with About and the Mission.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: Is DSPACE still in development?
text: Yes. The game is under active development, and new quests, systems, and improvements ship regularly. If you want to help shape what’s next, join the community on Discord or see the Roadmap.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: How do I get started?
text: Begin with the Welcome quest tree, which teaches the basics of accepting quests, completing objectives, and collecting rewards. The Quest Trees page lists every quest line so you can pick the next path once the tutorial is complete.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: What can I do?
text: There are a variety of quests to complete. Quests are the primary way to learn about the lore and mechanics of the game. If you're all caught up, try building your own adventure with the custom quest system or replay story arcs to see the alternate outcomes hidden behind optional objectives.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: How do I make progress?
text: Quests are the primary way to make progress in the game. They can grant certain items and introduce you to processes.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: What are quest trees?
text: Quest trees group related quests into themed learning paths such as 3D printing, energy systems, hydroponics, and rocketry. Browse the full catalog on the Quest Trees page to find a topic that interests you.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: What are processes and how do they work?
text: Processes are something you can do to convert one or more items into other items. This is the primary way to create items in the game.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: Where do items live and how does inventory work?
text: Your inventory holds everything you earn from quests and processes. Items are stored locally in IndexedDB, grouped into categories, and can be filtered or inspected in detail from the page.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: Can I create custom quests, items, or processes?
text: Yes. DSPACE includes in-game editors for all three: , , and . Edits are stored locally and can be managed from , , and . See the Content Development Guide for the full workflow.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: How do I submit custom quests?
text: Use the Quest Submission Guide to package your quest, process, or item so it can be reviewed for inclusion in the canon game. The guide walks through exporting your content, generating screenshots, and opening a pull request directly from the in-game tools.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: What are custom content bundles?
text: Bundles let you submit related quests, items, and processes together as one JSON file so they stay in sync. Learn the format and submission flow in Custom Content Bundles.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: Where is my save data stored?
text: Game state, custom content, achievements, and tokens are stored locally in your browser using IndexedDB. DSPACE does not send your data to a server unless you explicitly enable Cloud Sync.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: How do I back up or restore my data?
text: Use the export tools described in Backups to copy a Base64 snapshot of your gamesave or custom content. You can restore by pasting the saved string into the matching import screen.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: How does Cloud Sync work?
text: Cloud Sync stores encrypted backups in a private GitHub gist. You supply a GitHub token and gist ID in the Settings page, and DSPACE uploads changes automatically. Details are in Cloud Sync.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: Do I need a GitHub token?
text: A GitHub token is required only for features that interact with GitHub, such as submitting quests or using Cloud Sync. Tokens are stored locally and can be cleared at any time. See the Authentication Flow for setup and security notes.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: How do I log out or clear my token?
text: Open the Settings page and click Log out to clear the saved GitHub token and any Cloud Sync ID. You can also use the Clear button next to the token input field. See Authentication Flow for details.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: What are dUSD, dWatt, and dCarbon?
text: - dUSD is the in-game currency used for buying and selling. - dWatt tracks energy generation used to power processes. - dCarbon tracks emissions produced by non-renewable power sources.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: How do energy sources affect the economy?
text: Generating power from fossil-fuel outlets increases dCarbon. For every 1000 dCarbon, shop sell prices drop by 1% (up to 90%), so renewable energy like solar power or wind turbines is the best long-term strategy. See Electricity for the full rules.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: How do I reduce dCarbon?
text: Run the dCarbon to dOffset process to convert accumulated dCarbon into dOffset. The process consumes dCarbon and a small amount of dUSD, lowering your emissions footprint. See dCarbon.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: Where can I track achievements and titles?
text: Achievements live on and update as you finish quests or collect items. Titles are tied to achievements and appear on and your profile. See Achievements and Titles.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: What are guilds and the Metaguild?
text: Guilds are a planned multiplayer feature (2027+ on the roadmap). Once ActivityPub integration lands, players will be able to form guilds, coordinate shared progress, and unlock guild-specific content. The Metaguild will be the flagship guild, but it is not available yet. See Guilds for the current plan.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: How do I search the docs?
text: Use the search box on to find pages by title or content. Searches also support filters (for example, ). See Docs search.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: Can I self-host DSPACE?
text: Yes. DSPACE provides a Docker Compose setup for running the game locally. See Self-hosting DSPACE for the quick-start guide and advanced deployment links.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
heading: Where can I learn more?
text: On the Discord or here in the docs. You can also explore the Contribution Guide and Developer Resources.

title: First Aid
heading: First Aid
text: First aid quests focus on preparedness and calm response: assembling kits, treating minor injuries, and maintaining clean supplies.

title: First Aid
heading: What you learn
text: - How to stock and restock a basic first aid kit - How to clean wounds, dress cuts, and manage minor burns - How to practice CPR techniques with proper hygiene

title: First Aid
heading: Quest trailheads
text: - Assemble a First Aid Kit - Practice Basic Wound Care - Restock Your First Aid Kit - Treat a Minor Burn - Stop a Nosebleed - Practice Basic CPR

title: First Aid
heading: Key gear
text: - First aid kit stocked with adhesive bandages, sterile gauze pads, and antiseptic wipes - Nitrile gloves (pair) and biohazard waste bags for clean handling - CPR pocket mask and CPR training manikin for safe practice

title: First Aid
heading: Common pitfalls
text: - Using expired supplies or forgetting to restock after a quest - Skipping hand hygiene and glove changes between treatments - Practicing CPR without a barrier device or cleaning the manikin

title: First Aid
heading: Next steps
text: - Build a restock cadence and add reminders to your quest log. - Pair first aid readiness with shop safety in Woodworking.

title: Geothermal
heading: Geothermal
text: Geothermal quests focus on monitoring ground-loop systems and keeping heat pump infrastructure healthy over time.

title: Geothermal
heading: What you learn
text: - How to survey ground temperature and compare seasonal trends - How to read loop pressure, inlet/outlet temperatures, and deltas - How to keep sensors calibrated and air purged from the loop

title: Geothermal
heading: Quest trailheads
text: - Survey for Geothermal Heat - Log Ground Temperature - Calibrate Ground Sensor - Check Loop Pressure - Monitor Heat Pump Energy Use - Purge Air from Ground Loop

title: Geothermal
heading: Key gear
text: - Thermistor logging rig and digital multimeter for sensor verification - Temperature log CSV and temperature line chart to compare trends - Clean calibration notes stored with your ground-loop records

title: Geothermal
heading: Common pitfalls
text: - Sampling at inconsistent times, which hides seasonal patterns - Moving sensors between locations without noting offsets - Clearing air from the loop without logging before-and-after readings

title: Geothermal
heading: Next steps
text: - Keep a seasonal baseline and revisit it after each maintenance quest. - Compare your thermal performance to energy logs in Energy Systems.

title: Glossary
heading: DSPACE
text: Short for Democratized Space (sometimes stylized as democratized.space).

title: Glossary
heading: Built-in quest
text: A quest shipped with the game and stored in the source repository. Built-in quests are read-only in the in-game editors.

title: Glossary
heading: Built-in item
text: An item shipped with the game and stored in the source repository. Built-in items are read-only in the in-game editors.

title: Glossary
heading: Built-in process
text: A process shipped with the game and stored in the source repository. Built-in processes are read-only in the in-game editors.

title: Glossary
heading: Custom entity
text: A quest, item, or process created in-game by a player and stored in the player's browser (typically in IndexedDB, with a non-persistent in-memory fallback if IndexedDB is unavailable). Custom entities are editable, exportable, and can be submitted for inclusion in the official game.

title: Glossary
heading: Custom content bundle
text: A packaged set of related custom quests, items, and processes exported together for submission to the official game.

title: Glossary
heading: Custom content backup
text: The backup export/import workflow on /contentbackup used to package custom quests, items, and processes for transfer or recovery.

title: Glossary
heading: Custom item
text: An item created in-game and stored in the player's browser. Custom items are editable, exportable, and can be referenced by custom quests and custom processes.

title: Glossary
heading: Custom process
text: A process created in-game and stored in the player's browser. Custom processes are editable, exportable, and can reference both built-in and custom items.

title: Glossary
heading: Custom quest
text: A quest created in-game and stored in the player's browser. Custom quests are editable, exportable, and can reward items or trigger processes.

title: Glossary
heading: Guild
text: A planned multiplayer feature (2027+ on the roadmap) that will group players under the same banner. Guilds will have unique names and 2-4 character tags, with tags using any combination of the 26 letters of the English alphabet.

title: Glossary
heading: Hyperstructure
text: A hyperstructure is a set of protocols that adhere to the criteria listed in the jacob.energy article.

title: Glossary
heading: Inventory
text: The player's collection of items and currency balances. Inventory changes when quests grant items or when processes consume or create items.

title: Glossary
heading: Item
text: An object stored in the inventory. Items can be required or consumed by processes, created by processes, and granted as quest rewards.

title: Glossary
heading: NPC
text: Short for non-player character. NPCs deliver quest dialogue and anchor the narrative voice of questlines.

title: Glossary
heading: Process
text: An activity that transforms items into new ones. Processes can require items, consume items, and create items, making them the primary method for crafting objects, machines, decor, and plants.

title: Glossary
heading: Quest
text: A task that can be completed by the player. Quests use branching dialogue with an NPC and can grant or require items and processes.

title: Glossary
heading: Quest options
text: An actionable choice in a quest's dialogue flow. Options can jump to another step, finish a quest, start a process, or grant items.

title: Glossary
heading: In-game editor
text: The in-app UI for creating or updating custom quests, items, and processes. Editors store entries locally until they are exported or submitted.

title: Glossary
heading: Questline
text: A series of quests that are related to each other. Questlines are broken down into quests.

title: Guilds
heading: Guilds
text: Guilds are not live yet. They are planned for DSPACE v3 (2027+ on the roadmap) and will arrive after DSPACE integrates ActivityPub as the multiplayer foundation. This page outlines the intended direction so players know what to expect once guilds ship.

title: Guilds
heading: Planned guild experience
text:
When ActivityPub integration is complete, players will be able to:
 - Create or join guilds with a shared name, tag, and public profile. - Coordinate multiplayer progress through guild-specific questlines, shared milestones, and cooperative resource goals. - Unlock guild content that provides in-game benefits such as tooling access, bonus quest rewards, and specialized support. - Connect to the wider fediverse so guild identities, announcements, and updates can travel beyond a single DSPACE instance.

title: Guilds
heading: The Metaguild vision
text: The Metaguild will act as the flagship guild, providing a narrative anchor for newcomers and a place to collaborate on global tech tree goals. Specialist wings (print farm, hydroponics, rocketry, medicine, and more) will eventually host quests, checklists, and ongoing maintenance tasks for the whole crew.

title: Guilds
heading: What is not available today
text:
The following features are not implemented yet in the current build:
 - Guild creation, membership, or shared inventories - Guild-specific quests, rewards, or benefits - Donation boards, UBI funding controls, or live guild leaderboards - ActivityPub identities or cross-instance multiplayer coordination

title: Guilds
heading: How to follow progress
text: Keep an eye on the roadmap for milestone updates and join the Discord to discuss multiplayer planning. We will update this page as soon as guild development begins.

title: Hydroponics
heading: Hydroponics
text: Hydroponics is the practice of growing plants in nutrient-rich water instead of soil. In DSPACE, this skill ties together water treatment, sensor checks, and steady crop output.

title: Hydroponics
heading: What you learn
text: - How to assemble a hydroponic tub with pumps, net cups, and grow lights - How to manage pH, EC, and nutrient refresh cycles - How to sanitize equipment and keep roots healthy

title: Hydroponics
heading: Quest trailheads
text: - Install Submersible Pump - Install a Grow Light - Grow basil hydroponically - Check Solution pH - Calibrate EC Meter - Refresh Nutrient Solution

title: Hydroponics
heading: Key gear and materials
text: - Hydroponics kits, tubs, net cups, and submersible pumps - Nutrient concentrate, pH test strips, digital pH meters, and EC meters - Grow lights, air stones, and dechlorinated water

title: Hydroponics
heading: Good practices
text: - Log pH and EC readings every time you top off or refresh the reservoir. - Rinse and sanitize components between crops to prevent algae and salt buildup.

title: Inventory
heading: Inventory
text: Your inventory contains all the items you've collected through quests, processes, purchases, and other activities. In v3, inventory counts are stored in your browser using IndexedDB when it is available. Game state snapshots are also written to localStorage as a lightweight backup, and localStorage becomes the primary store if IndexedDB is unavailable.

title: Inventory
heading: Item Types
text:
The catalog spans several broad groupings, including:
 1. Resources - Energy units (dWatt, dSolar) - Currency (dUSD) - Raw materials (PLA filament, water)
 2. Tools and Equipment - 3D printers - Solar panels and batteries - Aquarium equipment - Hydroponics systems
 3. Components - 3D printed parts - Rocket components - Electronic components
 4. Consumables - Plant nutrients - Fish food - Rocket fuel

title: Inventory
heading: Inventory categories and filters
text:
The page groups items into filterable categories so you can narrow down large lists quickly. Categories are defined in the item JSON data under . The filter chips are derived directly from the loaded item catalog (built-in JSON plus any custom items), so category changes flow through without additional hardcoded lists.
 - 3D Printing & Fabrication - Astronomy & Observation - Biology & Lab - Digital & Records - Digital Currency & Tokens - Electronics & Robotics - Energy & Power - Rocketry & Flight - Safety & Medical - Transportation - Workshop & Construction - Plus existing categories for Aquarium, Awards, Hydroponics, and Tools
 Items missing an explicit category fall back to the "Uncategorized" label in the UI. Custom items default to the "Custom" category unless you supply a different one. When adding a new built-in category, update the relevant JSON entries and refresh the assertions in so the expected category set stays in sync.

title: Inventory
heading: Item Properties
text:
Each item definition includes:
 - Unique ID - Name - Description - Image - Price and unit (when tradeable) - Type and category metadata - Optional dependencies list
 Inventory counts live in your save data and are rendered alongside each item.

title: Inventory
heading: Item Relationships
text:
Items are interconnected through:
 1. Processes - Required items (checked but not consumed) - Consumed items (removed when process starts) - Created items (added when process completes)
 2. Quests - Requirements for dialogue options - Rewards for completion - Granted items during dialogue

title: Inventory
heading: Custom Items
text:
With v3, you can create custom items that:
 - Can be used in custom processes - Can be granted by custom quests - Are stored locally in the custom content database (with an in-memory fallback if IndexedDB is unavailable) - Can be exported via for sharing or submission

title: Inventory
heading: Item Management
text:
The inventory interface allows you to:
 - View item details and counts - Filter by category, search by ID/name/description/price, and sort by name, price, or count - Toggle "Show all items" to include zero-count entries - See related processes plus quest IDs on the item detail page - Preview custom item metadata (including dependencies) from the Manage Items page - Remove custom items you no longer need directly from the Manage Items page
 Inventory counts are stored locally using IndexedDB when available, while localStorage receives a parallel backup and becomes the fallback store if IndexedDB cannot be opened. Custom items, quests, and processes use the IndexedDB-backed custom content database with an in-memory fallback. For cross-device backups you can use Cloud Sync or export a custom content bundle from /contentbackup.

title: Inventory
heading: Browser Support
text: IndexedDB functionality is used for both game saves and custom content when the browser supports it. If IndexedDB is unavailable, inventory counts fall back to localStorage and custom content falls back to an in-memory store.

title: Item Development Guidelines
heading: Item Development Guidelines
text: This guide provides structured instructions for creating and managing custom items in DSPACE. Well-designed items enhance the game experience and expand the capabilities of processes and quests.

title: Item Development Guidelines
heading: Item Philosophy
text:
DSPACE items should:
 1. Serve a purpose - Connect to processes, quests, or educational concepts 2. Be realistic - Represent objects that could exist in a space exploration context 3. Have clear relationships - Establish connections to other game elements 4. Follow naming conventions - Use consistent, descriptive terminology 5. Include educational value - Teach concepts relevant to space exploration

title: Item Development Guidelines
heading: Item Categories
text: When creating items, consider these categories:

title: Item Development Guidelines
heading: Core Item Types
text: - Resources: Basic materials like energy units (dWatt), currency (dUSD), raw materials - Tools & Equipment: Devices used to perform processes (3D printers, sensors, etc.) - Components: Parts used in constructing larger systems (rocket parts, circuit boards) - Consumables: Items that get used up through processes (fuel, nutrients, filament) - Educational: Items that provide information or learning opportunities

title: Item Development Guidelines
heading: Basic Properties
text:
Every item requires the following basic properties:
 - name: Descriptive name of the item (required) - description: Detailed explanation of the item's purpose and use (required) - image: Visual representation of the item (required) - price: Value in game currency (optional). Enter a positive amount and select the currency; the form stores the combined string (e.g., ). - unit: Measurement unit for the item (e.g., kg, L, watts) (optional) - type: Classification or category (optional)

title: Item Development Guidelines
heading: Implementation State
text:
Currently, the component supports creating and editing items with the properties listed above. It uploads images, persists data to IndexedDB, and now includes a dependency editor so complex items can declare required ingredients without editing JSON manually.
 List any prerequisite item IDs in the Dependencies field using commas or new lines. The form trims whitespace, ignores blank rows, and the live preview immediately reflects the dependency list so you can verify relationships before saving.
 As you fill out the form, an component displays a live preview so you can confirm the details before submitting. The layout automatically adjusts on small screens so form fields expand to the full width for easier touch input, and action buttons stack vertically on narrow displays. You can safely experiment with different values before saving—no data is written until you click Create Item.
 The form provides inline validation messages if you attempt to submit without a name, description, or image, helping ensure items meet basic requirements before saving. Automated Playwright tests verify that the preview appears when entering text and that uploaded images render correctly. This ensures cross-browser compatibility of the custom item workflow.
 All items must now conform to the JSON schema located at . Run the test to ensure any additions meet the schema requirements.

title: Item Development Guidelines
heading: Naming Conventions
text: 1. Be specific - "Solar Panel (5W)" is better than "Solar Panel" 2. Include measurements - Add units when relevant (e.g., "Water Tank (10L)") 3. Use standard terminology - Research proper terms for scientific concepts

title: Item Development Guidelines
heading: Descriptions
text: 1. Explain purpose - What the item is used for 2. Include educational content - Brief facts about real-world equivalents 3. Connect to space context - How the item relates to space exploration 4. Keep it concise - 1-3 sentences is usually sufficient

title: Item Development Guidelines
heading: Images
text:
When you upload a custom item image in-game, the client downsamples it to a 512×512 JPEG and compresses it to target < 50KB. This keeps IndexedDB storage small, speeds up backups, and improves load times. Developers should reuse the shared helper at for any future custom image ingestion points.
 1. Clear representation - Should clearly show what the item is 2. Consistent style - Match the visual style of existing items 3. Appropriate format - Use PNG or JPG format, sized appropriately 4. Attribution - Include credits for images if required

title: Item Development Guidelines
heading: Item Relationships
text: Items in DSPACE are often connected to other game elements:

title: Item Development Guidelines
heading: Process Relationships
text: - Required by: Processes that need this item to run but don't consume it - Consumed by: Processes that use up this item - Created by: Processes that produce this item

title: Item Development Guidelines
heading: Quest Relationships
text:
- Required for: Quest dialogue options that need this item - Granted by: Quests that give this item as a reward or during dialogue
 When creating custom items, consider how they might connect to:
 1. Existing processes 2. Existing quests 3. New processes you're developing 4. New quests you're developing

title: Item Development Guidelines
heading: Contribution Workflow
text:
The recommended way to contribute items is through the custom content bundle workflow, which keeps related quests, items, and processes together:
 1. Create item through the in-game interface at /inventory/create 2. Export your created item as JSON from the management page: - After saving your item, navigate to /inventory/manage - Locate your newly created item in the list and use the export functionality to download the JSON - Save the exported JSON file - this will be included in your content bundle 3. Create any related processes at /processes/create and export them similarly 4. Create any related quests at /quests/create and export them similarly 5. Package everything into a bundle JSON (see Custom Content Bundles) 6. Submit the bundle for review at /bundles/submit 7. Respond to feedback on the generated pull request 8. Once approved, your content bundle becomes available to all players
 Alternatively, you can follow the traditional manual workflow:
 1. Develop your custom item(s) following these guidelines 2. Test the item(s) in various processes to ensure they function as expected 3. Create a content bundle JSON with related quests and processes 4. Submit a pull request with your bundle file in 5. Respond to feedback during code review 6. Once approved, your content will be merged into the official game

title: Item Development Guidelines
heading: Areas Needing More Content
text:
We're particularly interested in new items that cover:
 - Sustainable materials and recycling - Energy storage and generation - Life support systems - Scientific instruments - Plant cultivation tools - Water management systems - Educational models and demonstrations
 By following these guidelines, you'll create items that enhance gameplay while advancing DSPACE's mission of democratizing space exploration through education.

title: Legacy saves & storage migrations
heading: Legacy saves & storage migrations
text: This doc explains how DSPACE stores game state across v1 (cookies + localStorage), v2 (localStorage), and v3 (IndexedDB), plus how the legacy upgrade flow detects and migrates older formats.

title: Legacy saves & storage migrations
heading: Release timeline context (historical references)
text: - V1 → V2 transition (June 30, 2023): The v2 release notes call out returning v1 players and the quest reset, which anchors the cutoff between cookie-era saves and the v2 localStorage format. See . - V2.1 localStorage format (September 15, 2023): v2.1 explicitly documents that game state is stored in localStorage. See . - V3 IndexedDB migration (March 1, 2026): the changelog entry documents the IndexedDB-based storage system and legacy migration context. See .

title: Legacy saves & storage migrations
heading: DSPACE v1 save format (commit )
text:
High-level summary
 - Storage split: v1 writes inventory + quest progress into cookies and uses localStorage for process timers and machine locks. - v3 detection: v3 checks cookies/localStorage for legacy artifacts; the migration scope for v1 is items + currency balances (v1 cookies plus ). - Why skip quests/process timers: v1 quest IDs are numeric ( , , ), while v2/v3 use slug routes (ex: ). Process timers also depend on v1 process IDs/machine IDs that do not map 1:1. Treat quest/progress/process timers as out-of-scope for the default v1 migration, even if the cookies exist.

 - Cookie acceptance + redirect UX: - Cookie helpers + wallet balance functions: - Inventory cookie reads/writes: - Quest finish + checkpoint cookies: (contains ), (contains ) - Process timers + machine locks: - Process cooldown cookies: - Item catalog (source of truth):

title: Legacy saves & storage migrations
heading: QA: v1 → v3 migration checklist
text: 1. Start clean: clear IndexedDB + localStorage + cookies. Confirm no legacy banner appears. 2. Seed minimal v1 cookies (above), reload, and confirm the Legacy save detected banner appears on non-settings pages and links to . 3. Check V1 detection UI: in → Legacy save upgrades, confirm: - The V1 card lists item counts from cookies. - is surfaced as a dUSD balance and marked for migration. - Quest/progress cookies (if present) remain ignored by default. 4. Merge v1 into v3: click Merge v1 into current save. - Inventory gains the seeded items. - dUSD balances are migrated from . - The UI attempts to expire detected and cookies after the merge. If the cookies are cleared, the banner disappears after reload; if not, clear them manually. - If you re-seed the cookies and merge again, counts should increase again (merge is additive). 5. Maximal seed case: repeat with the maximal profile and confirm the same results, plus ensure process timers and machine locks remain ignored. 6. Hand-off to v2 → v3: after finishing v1 validation, continue with the v2 localStorage checklist in the section below.

title: Legacy saves & storage migrations
heading: Legacy v2 localStorage payloads (current codebase)
text:
v3 treats legacy v2 saves as localStorage JSON blobs stored under the or keys ( ). Parsing is handled by / in , which accepts either a direct JSON object or a wrapper containing a top-level field. A payload is treated as legacy if / starts with or , or if it does not start with and includes a legacy-shaped object with , , or .
 Normalization happens in : it keeps , , , and (when present) and sanitizes legacy inventory IDs through . This is the data shape consumed by the migration helpers in .

title: Legacy saves & storage migrations
heading: v2.1 QA fixtures (seeded from )
text: The QA cheats panel uses to seed for testing. The fixture includes three profiles (minimal, in-progress, messy) to exercise inventory remapping, quest/process preservation, and unknown keys. These fixtures are intentionally small, representative samples — real-world saves may include additional keys.

title: Legacy saves & storage migrations
heading: QA: v2 → v3 migration checklist
text: 1. Start clean: clear IndexedDB + localStorage. Confirm no legacy banner appears on v3. 2. Seed each v2 profile via the QA cheats panel (or manually set ), then reload and confirm → Legacy save upgrades reports legacy v2 data. 3. Merge into existing v3 save: validate additive inventory behavior and that quests/processes are preserved (no overwrites). 4. Replace into existing v3 save: validate replacement behavior and that the v3 save is written to IndexedDB after import. 5. Legacy data cleanup: removes and when IndexedDB is in use; confirm that behavior matches runtime storage mode (localStorage-only environments retain legacy keys).

heading: V2 storage (localStorage)
text:
Schema: A JSON object stored under a single key in v2.1.
 - Keys: (v2.1). is a v3-era fallback key but is still checked by v3 legacy detection. See the legacy v2 parsing section above for the authoritative schema. - Payload: JSON with beginning with (or a v2-shaped object containing , , and ), plus optional keys that are ignored during normalization unless they map to . - Example payload:

 - Detection + legacy parsing: ( ) and ( ). - Fixture seed data: . - Merge/replace logic: ( , ).

title: Legacy saves & storage migrations
heading: V3 storage (IndexedDB)
text:
Schema: The canonical v3 save lives in IndexedDB, with localStorage used as a lightweight backup and fallback for unsupported environments.
 - Database: (version ). - Object stores: , . - Key: (the game state blob is stored under a fixed key). - LocalStorage mirrors: and are still written after every save to provide a recovery path when IndexedDB is unavailable.
 Code references:
 - IndexedDB constants and store layout: ( , , , , ). - Persistence + fallback behavior: ( , , ).
 DevTools inspection:
 - Application tab → IndexedDB → → / → key . - Compare with localStorage fallback keys if IndexedDB is blocked (Safari private mode, etc.).

title: Legacy saves & storage migrations
heading: Detection & upgrade flow
text:
Detection logic:
 - V1 cookies are detected on the client with , which parses cookies plus for supported currency symbols (currently only via ). - V2 localStorage detection reads / , parses JSON safely, and accepts either a / prefix of / or a v2-shaped payload containing , , or . - Shared detection entry point: ( ).
 Merge vs. replace semantics:
 - V1 → V3: adds counts to the current inventory (merge) or starts from an empty validated state (replace). The UI calls with in the current codebase, so successful v1 imports grant the Early Adopter Token and the V2 Upgrade Trophy when legacy items are imported (see ). - V2 → V3: replaces the current save with the legacy state, and combines inventory while preserving existing quests/processes. - Both flows update to and persist to IndexedDB via in .
 Cleanup behavior:
 - V1 cleanup expires each detected and cookie (see ). Other legacy cookies (ex: , ) are not cleared. - V2 cleanup deletes / during v2 → v3 migrations (when IndexedDB is in use) and also via the Delete v2 localStorage action in the Legacy Save Upgrade UI. Both flows are best-effort (they skip removal if IndexedDB is unavailable or the browser is already using localStorage for the active save). - The Clear v3 save for testing action (available when QA cheats are enabled) only removes v3 persistence data; it preserves legacy v2 / entries if they still contain v2-formatted saves.

title: Legacy saves & storage migrations
heading: QA seeding
text:
QA seeding writes known-good fixtures that match the above schemas and is available when the QA cheats panel is enabled. QA cheat availability is controlled by via .
 - V1 seed: reads . - V2 seed: reads .
 The seeder exposes two v1 profiles (minimal + maximal) and three v2.1 profiles (minimal, in-progress, messy), and reports which keys were written after each seed action in the QA cheats panel summary.
 After seeding, use → Legacy save upgrades to merge or replace, and verify detection with the global legacy banner (shared detection logic).

title: Mission
heading: Mission
text: The mission is simple: to make space exploration and travel as accessible as possible. Economies of scale mean that space travel is currently only accessible to large companies and governments. DSPACE aims to change that.
